{
  "claims": [
    "The lack of paid disability leave contributes to problems with attracting and retaining educators in the workforce.",
    "Many educators are forced to leave the profession when they become pregnant and often do not return.",
    "Women educators earn almost $100,000 less in retirement than their male colleagues on average.",
    "AB 2901 would have granted public school employees up to 14 weeks of leave with full pay for pregnancies and pregnancy-related health issues.",
    "AB 2901 would have ended the discriminatory practice of requiring pregnant employees to deplete their sick leave.",
    "AB 2901 aligns with the International Labor Organization's recommendation of 14 weeks for maternity leave.",
    "AB 2901 aligns with the goals of the Paid Family Leave Task Force by increasing leave access and providing better job protections.",
    "Educators and community college faculty require necessary time off to care for themselves and their children."
  ]
}
